title: ortholoma cristatum
preparations: Ortholoma cristatum has many herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used for medicinal purposes. The tea made from Ortholoma cristatum is said to have anti-inflammatory properties and is used to treat digestive issues and reduce fever. A tincture of the plant is used to treat anxiety and insomnia due to its sedative effects, while an infusion of the leaves is used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. Additionally, a decoction of the roots is used to treat skin conditions and wounds due to its antimicrobial properties.
side_effects: Ortholoma cristatum can have side effects if used improperly, such as inducing nausea, dizziness, and headaches due to its high concentration of toxic compounds. In rare cases, ingesting large amounts of the plant can lead to respiratory failure and even death. Long-term exposure to its volatile oils has also been linked to skin irritation, allergic reactions, and liver damage. Additionally, using Ortholoma cristatum as a treatment for medical conditions without proper medical supervision can exacerbate underlying health issues and interact with other medications in unpredictable ways.
